03 2002 2001 2000 1999 1998 1997 1996 1995 1994 1993 1992 1991 1990 1989 1988 1987 1986 1985 1984 Skip to sidebar Quasi-Static Scheduling for Multiprocessor DSP Soonhoi Ha andEdward A. Lee Proceedings of IEEE International Symposium on Circuits and S

of IEEE International Symposium on Circuits and Systems , vol. 1, pp 352-355, Singapore, June, 1991. ABSTRACT Scheduling strategies for a multiprocessor DSP (digital signal processor) are classified into four types: fully dynamic, static-assignment,

into four types: fully dynamic, static-assignment, self-timed, and fully static. The concept of static scheduling (self-timed or fully static) is extended to handle nondeterministic actors in the proposed quasi-static scheduling. Quasi-static schedul

osed quasi-static scheduling. Quasi-static scheduling minimizes run-time overhead with increased compilation complexity, and is thus adequate for signal processing applications. The proposed scheme is applicable to the dynamic (static-assignment) sch
applicable to the dynamic (static-assignment) scheduling strategy as well as to static (self-timed) scheduling. Contact Update Help Copyright © 1999 - UC Regents; all rights reserved.
